articles.headline: India Vows 'Decisive Response' After Horrific Pahalgam Attack, Security High Across Borders
articles.explainer:
• Following a devastating terrorist attack in Pahalgam, Jammu & Kashmir, India condemns the act and signals a strong retaliation.
• The attack, targeting security forces and civilians, has led to a nationwide outcry and calls for immediate action from the government.
• High-level security meetings are underway, with the Prime Minister reviewing options for a robust response to the cross-border terrorism.
• Border areas, especially along the Line of Control (LoC), are on high alert with increased surveillance and troop deployment.
• The international community has been urged to unequivocally condemn the attack and the support for cross-border terrorism.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Border area management
India and its neighbourhood relations
prelimsPoints: Pahalgam is a town in Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir.
It is a popular tourist destination and base camp for the Amarnath Yatra.
The Line of Control (LoC) is a de facto boundary between India and Pakistan.
The National Security Council (NSC) advises the Prime Minister on matters of national security.
mainsPoints: Analyse the challenges posed by cross-border terrorism to India's internal security and regional stability.
Discuss India's evolving response strategy to major terror attacks, including diplomatic and military options.
Examine the role of international diplomacy and global counter-terrorism frameworks in addressing state-sponsored terrorism.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of recurring terrorist incidents on regions like Jammu and Kashmir.
articles.imageDescription: Indian security forces on high alert near the Line of Control.
articles.trivia: Pahalgam is often referred to as the 'Valley of Shepherds' and is famous for its scenic beauty and trekking routes.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Role of external state and non-state actors

articles.headline: Veteran Actor Madhav Vaze Passes Away at 85, Leaves Behind Rich Legacy
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Marathi and Hindi film and theatre actor Madhav Vaze passed away at the age of 85.
• Vaze was celebrated for his versatile performances, contributing significantly to Indian cinema and Marathi theatre.
• His notable works include the iconic Marathi film 'Shyamchi Aai' and a memorable role in the Bollywood blockbuster '3 Idiots'.
• Condolences have poured in from across the film fraternity, political leaders, and cultural figures, remembering his artistic contributions.
• He was a respected figure, known for his dedication to acting and his quiet influence on younger generations of artists.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Personalities in Indian arts
prelimsPoints: Madhav Vaze's notable films include 'Shyamchi Aai' and '3 Idiots'.
He worked predominantly in Marathi and Hindi cinema/theatre.
'Shyamchi Aai' is based on the autobiography of Sane Guruji.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contribution of regional cinema and theatre artists in enriching India's diverse cultural landscape.
Analyse the evolution of Indian theatre and its role in social commentary and cultural preservation.
Examine the impact of veteran actors like Madhav Vaze on the artistic and cultural ethos of the nation.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of veteran actor Madhav Vaze.
articles.trivia: Madhav Vaze was also known for his work as a director and playwright in Marathi theatre, further cementing his legacy in the performing arts.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has affirmed the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed petitions challenging various provisions of the Act, citing concerns over privacy and state surveillance.
• The court emphasized the need for a robust legal framework to protect citizens' data in the digital age while balancing national security interests.
• The judgment provides clarity on data fiduciary obligations, data principal rights, and the role of the Data Protection Board of India.
• This landmark decision is expected to accelerate the implementation of the Act and strengthen India's digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Information Technology and Computers
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act, 2023, is India's primary legislation for data protection.
It defines 'data fiduciary' (entity determining means of processing personal data) and 'data principal' (individual to whom data relates).
The Data Protection Board of India is the enforcement body.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights and state/corporate data processing needs.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a digitally diverse country like India.
Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on digital governance, innovation, and international data flows.
Analyse the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding fundamental rights in the context of emerging technologies.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The concept of data protection in India gained significant momentum after the S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) which recognized privacy as a fundamental right.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Digital Laws

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Focus on Inflation Management and Economic Stability
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach amidst persistent inflationary pressures and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor emphasized the commitment to bringing inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a continued focus on liquidity management.
• Analysts suggest the pause provides stability for businesses, but future rate actions will depend on evolving economic data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance means reducing excess liquidity in the system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives in the current global scenario.
Analyse the impact of an unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, real estate, and manufacturing.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and ensuring financial stability.
Examine the factors influencing the MPC's decisions and their implications for future economic outlook.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing Experiment for Future Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully conducted another crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) autonomously performed a precise landing on a simulated runway, validating key technologies.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a cost-effective and sustainable space transportation system for India.
• Reusable launch vehicles are crucial for reducing the cost of space access and increasing mission frequency.
• The program aims to develop fully reusable two-stage orbital (TSTO) launch vehicles in the long term.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD program aims to develop reusable launch vehicles.
The main goal of RLV is to reduce the cost of space launches.
India is pursuing RLV technology to become a major player in the global space market.
RLV-TD has previously demonstrated hypersonics flight and atmospheric re-entry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's future space exploration and commercial space sector.
Analyse the economic and technological advantages of reusable rockets compared to conventional expendable launch vehicles.
Examine how indigenous development of RLV technology aligns with India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in the space domain.
Evaluate the potential impact of RLV on India's position in the global space economy and its role in international collaborations.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) performing a landing.
articles.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program is an ambitious project aimed at mastering technologies for a fully reusable launch vehicle, which could revolutionize space travel costs.

articles.headline: New National Green Hydrogen Mission Targets for 2030 Unveiled, Boosting Renewable Energy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy has announced ambitious revised targets under the National Green Hydrogen Mission for 2030.
• The new targets aim to significantly increase India's green hydrogen production capacity and reduce reliance on fossil fuels.
• Focus includes developing green hydrogen production hubs, promoting research and development, and creating a robust ecosystem.
• This initiative is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
• The mission also seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources (like solar, wind) to electrolyse water.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
India has set a target of achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
Hydrogen is considered a clean fuel as its combustion produces only water.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen in India's energy transition and its potential to achieve climate change targets.
Analyse the technological, economic, and infrastructural challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international collaborations in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits, including job creation and industrial growth, from the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is distinct from grey hydrogen (produced from natural gas with carbon emissions) and blue hydrogen (natural gas with carbon capture).

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, Focus on AI Governance and Digital Public Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting the G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, bringing together global leaders to discuss critical digital issues.
• Key agenda items include establishing a framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance, ensuring ethical and responsible AI development.
• Discussions also focus on leveraging Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for inclusive economic growth and bridging the digital divide.
• India is championing a global consensus on data governance and cross-border data flows.
• The meeting aims to foster international cooperation for a secure, open, and reliable digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union (EU).
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems like Aadhaar, UPI that enable public and private services.
AI governance involves developing policies and regulations for the ethical and responsible use of AI.
India played a significant role in promoting DPI during its G20 presidency in 2023.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing leadership role in shaping global digital governance frameworks, particularly concerning AI and DPI.
Analyse the opportunities and challenges for international cooperation in establishing ethical AI governance and ensuring responsible AI deployment.
Examine how Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) can be leveraged to achieve inclusive growth and bridge the digital divide in developing nations.
Evaluate the implications of diverse national approaches to data governance on cross-border data flows and the global digital economy.
articles.imageDescription: Representatives from G20 nations attending a digital economy meeting.
articles.trivia: India's G20 presidency in 2023 significantly pushed the agenda for Digital Public Infrastructure, leading to a Global Digital Public Infrastructure Repository (GDPIR).

articles.headline: 'Poshan Vahini' Initiative Launched to Combat Malnutrition and Improve Health in Tribal Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Women and Child Development, launched the 'Poshan Vahini' initiative.
• The program aims to address severe malnutrition and improve health indicators among tribal communities in remote regions.
• It involves mobile health and nutrition units (Poshan Vahini) delivering supplementary nutrition, health check-ups, and awareness campaigns.
• The initiative focuses on pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children under five, utilizing community health workers.
• It seeks to integrate traditional tribal food practices with modern nutritional science for sustainable health outcomes.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: 'Poshan Vahini' is a joint initiative of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs and Ministry of Women and Child Development.
It targets tribal communities, focusing on pregnant women, lactating mothers, and young children.
Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) is the flagship program to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
Article 47 of the DPSP states the duty of the State to raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges leading to malnutrition in India's tribal areas and the specific vulnerabilities of these communities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of targeted interventions like 'Poshan Vahini' in improving health and nutritional outcomes among marginalized populations.
Analyse the role of inter-ministerial coordination and community participation in the successful implementation of welfare schemes.
Examine how traditional knowledge and food practices can be integrated with modern nutritional strategies for sustainable health solutions in tribal regions.
articles.imageDescription: A mobile health van (Poshan Vahini) reaching a remote tribal village.
articles.trivia: Many tribal communities possess rich traditional knowledge about nutritious local foods and medicinal plants, which can be integrated into modern health programs.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Health and Nutrition for Vulnerable Sections

articles.headline: Cyber Swachhta Kendra 2.0 Launched to Boost National Cybersecurity and Combat Digital Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has launched 'Cyber Swachhta Kendra 2.0' as an enhanced version of the existing botnet cleaning and malware analysis centre.
• The revamped platform aims to provide advanced tools and resources for citizens and organizations to detect and remove malware.
• It integrates AI-powered threat intelligence and real-time alerts to proactively counter evolving cyber threats.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen its cybersecurity posture and promote digital safety.
• It also emphasizes public awareness campaigns on safe browsing practices and reporting cyber incidents.
gsSubTopics: Cyber security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
'Cyber Swachhta Kendra' (Botnet Cleaning and Malware Analysis Centre) was initially launched in 2017.
It aims to create a secure cyber ecosystem in India.
Botnets are networks of compromised computers controlled by a third party.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India and the challenges in protecting critical digital infrastructure and citizen data.
Evaluate the role of initiatives like 'Cyber Swachhta Kendra 2.0' in enhancing India's cybersecurity capabilities and promoting digital resilience.
Analyse the importance of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in combating sophisticated cyberattacks.
Examine the ethical and privacy concerns associated with large-scale data collection for threat intelligence and its implications for individual rights.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting cybersecurity threats and a shield protecting digital data.
articles.trivia: CERT-In also issues advisories and guidelines for cybersecurity best practices to individuals and organizations across India.

articles.headline: Phase III of Bharatmala Pariyojana Approved, Focus on Economic Corridors and Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its approval for the third phase of the ambitious Bharatmala Pariyojana, a flagship highway development program.
• Phase III will primarily focus on developing high-speed economic corridors and inter-corridor connectivity to enhance freight movement.
• The project aims to improve logistics efficiency, reduce transportation costs, and boost economic activities across various regions.
• It includes construction of new expressways, widening of existing national highways, and integration with multimodal logistics parks.
• The initiative is expected to generate significant employment opportunities and accelerate regional development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
articles.source: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
prelimsPoints: Bharatmala Pariyojana is a centrally sponsored and funded road and highway project.
It aims to optimize the efficiency of freight and passenger movement across the country.
Economic corridors are designed to connect major economic hubs.
Multimodal Logistics Parks integrate various modes of transport (road, rail, air, water) for efficient logistics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of robust road infrastructure in fostering economic growth and regional connectivity in India.
Analyse how projects like Bharatmala Pariyojana contribute to improving logistics efficiency and reducing supply chain costs for industries.
Evaluate the environmental and social challenges associated with large-scale highway development projects and measures to mitigate them.
Examine the role of infrastructure development in achieving India's vision of becoming a $5 trillion economy.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a newly constructed expressway under the Bharatmala Pariyojana.
articles.trivia: The Bharatmala Pariyojana is the second largest highway project in India after the National Highway Development Project (NHDP).

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Kisan Samridhhi Abhiyan' for Integrated Farmer Income Doubling Strategy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare has launched the 'Kisan Samridhhi Abhiyan' as a comprehensive strategy to double farmers' income.
• The initiative integrates various existing schemes and introduces new interventions focusing on productivity enhancement, value addition, and market access.
• Key pillars include promoting sustainable farming practices, crop diversification, post-harvest management, and direct market linkages.
• It emphasizes the role of Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) and digital platforms for better price realization.
• The Abhiyan aims to uplift the economic status of small and marginal farmers across the country.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: The goal of doubling farmers' income by 2022 was initially set by the government (now extended/re-emphasized).
Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) are groups of farmers who pool their resources for better market access and bargaining power.
E-NAM (National Agriculture Market) is an online trading platform for agricultural commodities.
Sustainable farming practices aim to maintain ecological balance and long-term productivity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges faced by Indian agriculture in achieving the goal of doubling farmers' income.
Analyse how integrated strategies like 'Kisan Samridhhi Abhiyan' can address issues of productivity, market access, and value addition for farmers.
Evaluate the role of technology, FPOs, and policy support in transforming the agricultural sector and ensuring food security.
Examine the impact of climate change and market volatility on farmer livelihoods and the need for resilient agricultural practices.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with modern agricultural machinery.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, but a significant portion of its farming population consists of small and marginal farmers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and schemes for agriculture

articles.headline: India Achieves Significant Milestone in Eliminating Lymphatic Filariasis Ahead of Global Targets
articles.explainer:
• India has made remarkable progress in its fight against Lymphatic Filariasis (LF), also known as 'Hathipaon' or elephantiasis, achieving elimination targets in several endemic districts.
• This milestone positions India ahead of the global target for LF elimination set by the World Health Organization (WHO).
• The success is attributed to mass drug administration (MDA) campaigns, enhanced surveillance, and vector control measures.
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare lauded the collaborative efforts of state governments, health workers, and communities.
• The achievement has significant public health implications, reducing disability and improving the quality of life for millions.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Lymphatic Filariasis is a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD).
It is caused by parasitic worms transmitted by mosquitoes.
Mass Drug Administration (MDA) is a key strategy for LF elimination.
India's target was to eliminate LF as a public health problem by 2027 (ahead of WHO's 2030 target).
mainsPoints: Discuss the public health challenges posed by Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) like Lymphatic Filariasis in India.
Analyse the strategies and interventions that have contributed to India's success in controlling and eliminating LF.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of LF on affected communities and the benefits of its elimination.
Examine the role of national health programs, international collaborations, and community participation in achieving disease elimination goals.
articles.imageDescription: A health worker administering medicine during a mass drug administration campaign.
articles.trivia: Lymphatic Filariasis is one of the oldest and most debilitating tropical diseases, causing severe disfigurement and long-term disability.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Disease Control and Public Health Initiatives

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Vocational Training Integration from School to Higher Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced significant progress in integrating vocational training across all levels of education, as envisioned by NEP 2020.
• New guidelines have been issued for schools to introduce vocational subjects from Class 6 onwards, including practical skills and internships.
• Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) are mandated to offer credit-based vocational courses and align curricula with industry needs.
• The move aims to enhance employability, reduce skill gaps, and foster an entrepreneurial mindset among students.
• Collaboration with industry partners and skill development councils is being strengthened to ensure relevance and quality of training.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 proposes vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
The policy aims to integrate vocational and academic streams.
Skill India Mission is a government initiative to train 40 crore people in various skills by 2022 (ongoing).
The National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) organizes qualifications according to a series of levels of knowledge, skills, and aptitude.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of vocational training integration in achieving the goals of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and addressing India's demographic dividend.
Analyse the challenges in implementing vocational education at scale, including infrastructure, faculty training, and industry collaboration.
Evaluate how a strong vocational education system can enhance youth employability, reduce skill gaps, and foster economic growth.
Examine the role of technology and innovative pedagogies in making vocational training more accessible and effective across diverse student populations.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in practical vocational training in a workshop.
articles.trivia: Globally, countries like Germany and Switzerland have highly successful dual vocational training systems that combine classroom learning with on-the-job experience.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: National Education Policy and Skill Development

articles.headline: ASI Discovers Ancient Buddhist Site in Odisha, Offering New Insights into Kalinga Period
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has unearthed a significant ancient Buddhist site in the Dhauli-Shishupalgarh region of Odisha.
• The discovery includes remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures, dating back to the Kalinga period and early medieval era.
• This finding is expected to provide valuable insights into the spread of Buddhism in Eastern India and the cultural practices of the time.
• Excavations reveal extensive monastic complexes and artifacts, suggesting a thriving Buddhist center.
• Experts highlight the site's potential to enhance understanding of ancient trade routes and socio-religious history of the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: Dhauli is famous for Ashoka's rock edicts, near Bhubaneswar, Odisha.
Shishupalgarh is an ancient fortified city in Odisha.
Stupas are Buddhist commemorative mounds, while viharas are Buddhist monasteries.
Kalinga was an ancient kingdom located on the east coast of India, corresponding to modern Odisha.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding the evolution of religious practices.
Analyse the contribution of Buddhism to India's art, architecture, and philosophical traditions, particularly in regions like Kalinga.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in preserving and promoting newly discovered heritage sites for tourism and education.
Examine the role of institutions like ASI in safeguarding India's cultural heritage and promoting historical research.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient Buddhist site in Odisha, revealing remnants of structures.
articles.trivia: The Kalinga War (c. 261 BCE) fought by Emperor Ashoka is a pivotal event in Indian history, leading to his conversion to Buddhism.

articles.headline: Editorial: The Geopolitical Implications of Arctic Council Expansion and India's Role
articles.explainer:
• The editorial discusses the growing geopolitical significance of the Arctic region and the potential expansion of the Arctic Council.
• It analyses the implications of new members or observers on the existing power dynamics and resource exploitation.
• India's increasing scientific and strategic interest in the Arctic, as an observer state, is highlighted.
• The editorial emphasizes the need for a balanced approach to Arctic governance, prioritizing environmental protection alongside economic opportunities.
• It calls for India to play a more proactive role in shaping sustainable policies for the rapidly changing polar region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Arctic Council is a high-level intergovernmental forum promoting cooperation, coordination, and interaction among the Arctic states.
Its eight member states are Canada, Denmark (Greenland & Faroe Islands), Finland, Iceland, Norway, Russia, Sweden, and the United States.
India has observer status in the Arctic Council since 2013.
India operates the Himadri research station in Svalbard, Norway.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing geopolitical and economic significance of the Arctic region in the 21st century, considering climate change and resource potential.
Analyse India's strategic interests and scientific engagements in the Arctic, despite not being a littoral state.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for Arctic governance amidst competing national interests and the need for environmental protection.
Examine how India can leverage its observer status in the Arctic Council to promote sustainable development and peaceful cooperation in the region.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Arctic region with the Arctic Council member states highlighted.
articles.trivia: The melting of Arctic ice opens up new shipping routes, such as the Northern Sea Route, potentially reducing travel time between Europe and Asia.

articles.headline: Wetland Conservation: Ramsar Sites in India See New Additions, Boosting Biodiversity Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the designation of three new Ramsar sites in India.
• These newly added wetlands are recognized for their international importance in terms of biodiversity and ecological functions.
• The expansion of Ramsar sites reinforces India's commitment to wetland conservation and sustainable management.
• Wetlands play a crucial role in water purification, flood control, carbon sequestration, and supporting diverse ecosystems.
• The government emphasized community participation and integrated management plans for the effective protection of these vital habitats.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Ecology
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is an intergovernmental treaty for the conservation and wise use of wetlands.
It was signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971.
India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention.
Wetlands are vital for migratory birds and support unique flora and fauna.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of wetlands and the threats they face from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyse the role of international conventions like Ramsar and national policies in promoting wetland conservation and sustainable use in India.
Evaluate the challenges in managing Ramsar sites effectively, including encroachment, pollution, and lack of community awareness.
Examine the socio-economic benefits derived from healthy wetland ecosystems, such as livelihood support, water security, and disaster risk reduction.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds, representing a newly designated Ramsar site.
articles.trivia: The world's first Ramsar site was the Cobourg Peninsula in Australia, designated in 1974.

articles.headline: India's Services PMI Hits Record High, Signalling Robust Economic Growth and Strong Demand
articles.explainer:
• India's Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to a record high in April, indicating robust growth in the services sector.
• The strong performance is driven by a significant increase in new business orders and sustained consumer demand.
• This positive trend reflects underlying economic resilience and strong recovery momentum post-pandemic.
• Employment in the services sector also saw a healthy increase, contributing to overall job creation.
• The upbeat PMI data suggests positive implications for India's GDP growth in the coming quarters.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: The Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) is an indicator of economic trends in the manufacturing and services sectors.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
The services sector is the largest contributor to India's GDP.
PMI is a leading indicator, providing insights into future economic activity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the robust growth of India's services sector and its significance for overall economic development.
Analyse the implications of a high Services PMI on employment generation, investment, and consumer sentiment.
Evaluate the challenges that the services sector might face, such as global economic slowdowns, inflationary pressures, and skill shortages.
Examine how government policies can further support the growth of the services sector and enhance its contribution to India's GDP.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing the upward trend of India's Services PMI.
articles.trivia: The services sector in India includes diverse activities such as IT, financial services, healthcare, tourism, and real estate.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission for North-East States Concludes Work, Recommendations Submitted
articles.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission, constituted for several North-Eastern states, has concluded its work and submitted its final recommendations.
• The commission's mandate was to redraw the boundaries of assembly and parliamentary constituencies based on the latest census data.
• The recommendations aim to ensure fair representation and adhere to the 'one person, one vote' principle, considering geographical and demographic changes.
• The process involved extensive public consultations and engagements with political parties and civil society organizations.
• The implementation of these recommendations is expected to impact the political landscape and electoral dynamics in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Delimitation is the act of redrawing boundaries of Lok Sabha and state Assembly constituencies.
The Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India and works in collaboration with the Election Commission of India.
Its orders have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.
The last nationwide delimitation was completed in 2002-2008 based on the 2001 Census (with a freeze until 2026 for Lok Sabha seats).
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and objectives behind the delimitation process in India and its importance for democratic representation.
Analyse the specific challenges faced by the Delimitation Commission in the North-Eastern states, considering their unique geographical, demographic, and ethnic diversity.
Evaluate the potential political and social implications of the new delimitation recommendations on the electoral landscape and regional harmony.
Examine the role of the Election Commission of India and the Delimitation Commission in ensuring free and fair elections and equitable representation.
articles.imageDescription: A map of North-Eastern states with superimposed constituency boundaries.
articles.trivia: The Delimitation Commission has been constituted four times in India – in 1952, 1963, 1973, and 2002.

articles.headline: India, Brazil, South Africa (IBSA) Trilateral Dialogue Focuses on South-South Cooperation and Climate Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The IBSA (India, Brazil, South Africa) Trilateral Commission met to strengthen South-South cooperation across various sectors.
• Discussions focused on enhancing collaboration in trade, investment, sustainable development, and climate resilience initiatives.
• The leaders reiterated their commitment to reforming multilateral institutions and promoting a more equitable global order.
• India emphasized its commitment to sharing development experiences and technological solutions with partner countries.
• The dialogue underscored the importance of collective action by developing nations to address global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: IBSA is a unique forum bringing together three large democracies and major economies from three different continents.
It promotes South-South cooperation and coordination on global issues.
The IBSA Fund for the Alleviation of Poverty and Hunger supports development projects in other developing countries.
Brazil, India, and South Africa are also members of BRICS.
mainsPoints: Discuss the relevance and significance of platforms like IBSA in promoting South-South cooperation and strengthening the voice of developing countries on the global stage.
Analyse the potential for increased collaboration among IBSA nations in areas such as trade, technology transfer, and sustainable development.
Evaluate the role of IBSA in advocating for reforms of multilateral institutions and shaping a more inclusive global governance architecture.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging its partnerships within IBSA to advance its strategic and developmental interests.
articles.imageDescription: The flags of India, Brazil, and South Africa displayed during the IBSA meeting.
articles.trivia: The IBSA Dialogue Forum was formally established with the Brasilia Declaration in June 2003.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Sees Major Expansion, Boosting Grassroots Innovation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry announced a significant expansion of the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS).
• The enhanced scheme will provide financial assistance to a larger number of early-stage startups across various sectors.
• The expansion aims to foster grassroots innovation, encourage entrepreneurship in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, and bridge funding gaps.
• Focus includes supporting startups in emerging technologies, sustainable solutions, and social impact ventures.
• The initiative is crucial for nurturing a vibrant startup ecosystem and creating job opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market-entry, and commercialization.
It is implemented by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT).
Startup India is a flagship initiative of the Government of India launched in 2016.
A 'startup' is defined based on its age, turnover, and innovation/scalability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of seed funding and government support in fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem in India.
Analyse the challenges faced by early-stage startups in securing funding and scaling their operations, especially in non-metro cities.
Evaluate how schemes like SISFS contribute to inclusive growth, job creation, and technological innovation across diverse sectors.
Examine the role of government policies in creating an enabling environment for entrepreneurship and promoting India as a global startup hub.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space.
articles.trivia: India has emerged as the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

articles.headline: Ganga Rejuvenation: Phase II of Namami Gange Mission Approved with Focus on Tributaries
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod for the second phase of the Namami Gange Mission, extending its scope and duration.
• Phase II will place a significant emphasis on cleaning and rejuvenating the tributaries of the River Ganga, which are crucial for the main river's health.
• The mission will continue its focus on wastewater treatment infrastructure, industrial pollution abatement, and biodiversity conservation.
• Community participation and a holistic approach to river basin management are key components of the expanded mission.
• The aim is to ensure sustained ecological flow and overall health of the Ganga river system.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Programme is an Integrated Conservation Mission, approved by the Union Government in June 2014.
It aims at effective abatement of pollution, conservation, and rejuvenation of National River Ganga.
The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) is the implementing agency.
Ganga is the longest river in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges in cleaning and rejuvenating the Ganga river system, including industrial pollution, municipal waste, and agricultural runoff.
Analyse the key strategies and interventions under the Namami Gange Mission, evaluating their effectiveness in achieving pollution abatement and ecological restoration.
Examine the importance of a holistic river basin management approach, including the rejuvenation of tributaries, for the sustained health of major rivers.
Evaluate the role of public awareness, community participation, and inter-state cooperation in the success of large-scale river conservation projects.
articles.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the River Ganga with ghats, symbolizing rejuvenation efforts.
articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world, supporting over 500 million people.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Joint Maritime Security Exercise Concludes, Enhancing Bilateral Naval Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The joint maritime security exercise between India and Sri Lanka successfully concluded in the Indian Ocean region.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, sharing best practices, and strengthening coordinated responses to maritime threats.
• Activities included anti-piracy drills, search and rescue operations, and communication exercises.
• This collaboration underscores the commitment of both nations to ensuring peace and security in the Indian Ocean.
• The exercise also facilitated greater understanding and cooperation between the Indian Navy and the Sri Lanka Navy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India and Sri Lanka share a close maritime border in the Indian Ocean.
The Indian Ocean Region is strategically important for global trade and security.
Joint military exercises enhance mutual understanding and operational capabilities between forces.
SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region) is India's vision for maritime cooperation in the IOR.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of maritime security cooperation between India and its immediate neighbours in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyse how joint naval exercises contribute to enhancing regional stability, countering non-traditional security threats, and building mutual trust.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in India's bilateral relations with Sri Lanka, particularly in the context of maritime domain awareness and counter-terrorism.
Examine India's broader vision for security and growth in the Indian Ocean Region and its role as a net security provider.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka participating in a joint exercise at sea.
articles.trivia: India and Sri Lanka also have a joint military exercise called 'Mitra Shakti' focusing on counter-terrorism and counter-insurgency operations.

articles.headline: India Inaugurates Advanced Solar Telescope in Ladakh to Study Solar Activity and Space Weather
articles.explainer:
• India has inaugurated a state-of-the-art solar telescope at the Indian Astronomical Observatory (IAO) in Hanle, Ladakh.
• The telescope is designed to conduct high-resolution observations of the Sun's atmosphere and dynamic phenomena like solar flares and coronal mass ejections.
• This facility will significantly boost India's capabilities in solar physics research and space weather forecasting.
• Located at an altitude of over 4,500 meters, the site offers excellent atmospheric conditions for astronomical observations.
• The data collected will be crucial for understanding the Sun's impact on Earth's climate and satellite communications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: The Indian Astronomical Observatory (IAO) is located in Hanle, Ladakh.
Ladakh is known for its high-altitude, dry climate, and clear skies, ideal for astronomy.
Solar flares are sudden bursts of radiation from the Sun's surface.
Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs) are large expulsions of plasma and magnetic field from the Sun's corona.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of solar observations and space weather forecasting for technological infrastructure on Earth and space missions.
Analyse how advanced astronomical facilities like the new solar telescope enhance India's scientific research capabilities and global contributions in space science.
Evaluate the challenges and advantages of establishing high-altitude observatories in regions like Ladakh.
Examine the interdisciplinary nature of solar physics, linking it to Earth's climate, geomagnetic storms, and satellite operations.
articles.imageDescription: An image of the advanced solar telescope pointing towards the sky in the mountainous landscape of Ladakh.
articles.trivia: The Indian Astronomical Observatory in Hanle is one of the world's highest optical telescope sites.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023', consider the following statements:
1. It defines 'data fiduciary' as an individual to whom the personal data relates.
2. The Data Protection Board of India is the enforcement body for the Act.
3. The Act was enacted following the recognition of the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right by the Supreme Court.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. 'Data Fiduciary' is an entity determining the means and purpose of processing personal data. 'Data Principal' is the individual to whom the personal data relates. Statement 2 is correct. The Data Protection Board of India is established as the enforcement body. Statement 3 is correct. The DPDP Act was enacted to provide for processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process such personal data for lawful purposes, following the landmark Puttaswamy judgment (2017) which declared privacy a fundamental right.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes Green Hydrogen?
options: (a) Hydrogen produced from fossil fuels with carbon capture technology.
(b) Hydrogen produced using renewable energy sources through electrolysis of water.
(c) Hydrogen produced from natural gas without any carbon emission mitigation.
(d) Hydrogen derived from nuclear energy for industrial applications.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Hydrogen produced using renewable energy sources through electrolysis of water.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Green hydrogen is specifically produced through the electrolysis of water using electricity generated from renewable energy sources like solar or wind power, resulting in zero greenhouse gas emissions. Option (a) describes 'blue hydrogen', (c) describes 'grey hydrogen', and (d) could be a form of 'pink hydrogen' or simply hydrogen from nuclear, but 'green' specifically implies renewable sources.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Bharatmala Pariyojana':
1. It is a centrally sponsored and funded road and highway project.
2. Its primary objective is to develop economic corridors and improve logistics efficiency.
3. It is the largest highway project in India in terms of length.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Bharatmala Pariyojana is a centrally sponsored and funded project. Statement 2 is correct. Its core objective is to optimize freight and passenger movement, primarily through economic corridors and inter-corridor connectivity. Statement 3 is incorrect. The National Highway Development Project (NHDP) is considered the largest highway project in India, preceding Bharatmala Pariyojana.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)'s Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program, what is its primary objective?
options: (a) To develop intercontinental ballistic missiles for defense purposes.
(b) To establish a permanent human habitat on the Moon.
(c) To significantly reduce the cost of space access and enable sustainable space missions.
(d) To explore exoplanets beyond our solar system using advanced telescopes.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To significantly reduce the cost of space access and enable sustainable space missions.
prelimsMocks.explanation: ISRO's RLV program, like similar initiatives globally, aims to develop vehicles that can be used multiple times, thereby drastically cutting down the cost of launching satellites and other payloads into space. This makes space access more affordable and frequent, contributing to sustainable space exploration. Options (a), (b), and (d) are unrelated or secondary objectives not directly linked to the RLV program's primary goal.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following international organizations is primarily focused on the conservation and wise use of wetlands?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources. UNESCO focuses on education, science, culture; IUCN on nature conservation and sustainable use of natural resources; CITES on regulating international trade in endangered species.

mainsMocks.question: Analyse the multi-faceted challenges posed by cross-border terrorism to India's national security and regional stability. Discuss India's evolving response strategies, including diplomatic and military options, to counter such threats effectively. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security, Linkages between development and spread of extremism; Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security; Border area management. GS2: India and its neighbourhood relations.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce cross-border terrorism as a persistent threat. Discuss challenges like proxy warfare, intelligence gathering, radicalization, and regional instability. Detail India's response: diplomatic (global forums, bilateral pressure, sanctions), military (surgical strikes, border fencing, counter-insurgency ops), and internal (intelligence, community engagement, economic development in affected areas). Conclude with the need for a comprehensive, multi-pronged approach.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, is a crucial step towards safeguarding citizen privacy in the digital age. Critically evaluate the balance struck by the Act between individual privacy rights and the legitimate data processing needs of the state and corporations. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Act and its objective. Discuss how it protects individual rights (consent, right to access, correction, erasure) and imposes obligations on data fiduciaries. Critically evaluate provisions related to state exemptions (national security, public order), consent mechanisms, and cross-border data flows. Argue whether the balance adequately protects privacy while enabling innovation and governance. Conclude with suggestions for robust implementation and oversight.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen in India's energy transition and its potential to achieve climate change targets. What are the technological, economic, and infrastructural challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India, and how can they be addressed? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Define green hydrogen and its importance for decarbonization, energy security, and net-zero targets. Explain its role in hard-to-abate sectors. Detail challenges: high production cost (electrolysers, renewable energy), lack of infrastructure (storage, transport), technological advancements needed, policy support, and R&D. Suggest solutions: subsidies, R&D investment, international collaboration, creating demand, building dedicated infrastructure, and supportive regulatory frameworks. Conclude with India's potential as a global green hydrogen leader.

GS1.headline: Veteran Actor Madhav Vaze Passes Away at 85, Leaves Behind Rich Legacy
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Marathi and Hindi film and theatre actor Madhav Vaze passed away at the age of 85.
• Vaze was celebrated for his versatile performances, contributing significantly to Indian cinema and Marathi theatre.
• His notable works include the iconic Marathi film 'Shyamchi Aai' and a memorable role in the Bollywood blockbuster '3 Idiots'.
• Condolences have poured in from across the film fraternity, political leaders, and cultural figures, remembering his artistic contributions.
• He was a respected figure, known for his dedication to acting and his quiet influence on younger generations of artists.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Personalities in Indian arts
prelimsPoints: Madhav Vaze's notable films include 'Shyamchi Aai' and '3 Idiots'.
He worked predominantly in Marathi and Hindi cinema/theatre.
'Shyamchi Aai' is based on the autobiography of Sane Guruji.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contribution of regional cinema and theatre artists in enriching India's diverse cultural landscape.
Analyse the evolution of Indian theatre and its role in social commentary and cultural preservation.
Examine the impact of veteran actors like Madhav Vaze on the artistic and cultural ethos of the nation.
GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of veteran actor Madhav Vaze.
GS1.trivia: Madhav Vaze was also known for his work as a director and playwright in Marathi theatre, further cementing his legacy in the performing arts.

GS1.headline: ASI Discovers Ancient Buddhist Site in Odisha, Offering New Insights into Kalinga Period
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has unearthed a significant ancient Buddhist site in the Dhauli-Shishupalgarh region of Odisha.
• The discovery includes remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures, dating back to the Kalinga period and early medieval era.
• This finding is expected to provide valuable insights into the spread of Buddhism in Eastern India and the cultural practices of the time.
• Excavations reveal extensive monastic complexes and artifacts, suggesting a thriving Buddhist center.
• Experts highlight the site's potential to enhance understanding of ancient trade routes and socio-religious history of the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: Dhauli is famous for Ashoka's rock edicts, near Bhubaneswar, Odisha.
Shishupalgarh is an ancient fortified city in Odisha.
Stupas are Buddhist commemorative mounds, while viharas are Buddhist monasteries.
Kalinga was an ancient kingdom located on the east coast of India, corresponding to modern Odisha.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding the evolution of religious practices.
Analyse the contribution of Buddhism to India's art, architecture, and philosophical traditions, particularly in regions like Kalinga.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in preserving and promoting newly discovered heritage sites for tourism and education.
Examine the role of institutions like ASI in safeguarding India's cultural heritage and promoting historical research.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient Buddhist site in Odisha, revealing remnants of structures.
GS1.trivia: The Kalinga War (c. 261 BCE) fought by Emperor Ashoka is a pivotal event in Indian history, leading to his conversion to Buddhism.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has affirmed the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed petitions challenging various provisions of the Act, citing concerns over privacy and state surveillance.
• The court emphasized the need for a robust legal framework to protect citizens' data in the digital age while balancing national security interests.
• The judgment provides clarity on data fiduciary obligations, data principal rights, and the role of the Data Protection Board of India.
• This landmark decision is expected to accelerate the implementation of the Act and strengthen India's digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Information Technology and Computers
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act, 2023, is India's primary legislation for data protection.
It defines 'data fiduciary' (entity determining means of processing personal data) and 'data principal' (individual to whom data relates).
The Data Protection Board of India is the enforcement body.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights and state/corporate data processing needs.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a digitally diverse country like India.
Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on digital governance, innovation, and international data flows.
Analyse the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding fundamental rights in the context of emerging technologies.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The concept of data protection in India gained significant momentum after the S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) which recognized privacy as a fundamental right.

GS2.explainer:

• Key agenda items include establishing a framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance, ensuring ethical and responsible AI development.
• Discussions also focus on leveraging Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for inclusive economic growth and bridging the digital divide.
• India is championing a global consensus on data governance and cross-border data flows.
• The meeting aims to foster international cooperation for a secure, open, and reliable digital economy.

GS2.headline: 'Poshan Vahini' Initiative Launched to Combat Malnutrition and Improve Health in Tribal Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Women and Child Development, launched the 'Poshan Vahini' initiative.
• The program aims to address severe malnutrition and improve health indicators among tribal communities in remote regions.
• It involves mobile health and nutrition units (Poshan Vahini) delivering supplementary nutrition, health check-ups, and awareness campaigns.
• The initiative focuses on pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children under five, utilizing community health workers.
• It seeks to integrate traditional tribal food practices with modern nutritional science for sustainable health outcomes.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: 'Poshan Vahini' is a joint initiative of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs and Ministry of Women and Child Development.
It targets tribal communities, focusing on pregnant women, lactating mothers, and young children.
Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) is the flagship program to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
Article 47 of the DPSP states the duty of the State to raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges leading to malnutrition in India's tribal areas and the specific vulnerabilities of these communities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of targeted interventions like 'Poshan Vahini' in improving health and nutritional outcomes among marginalized populations.
Analyse the role of inter-ministerial coordination and community participation in the successful implementation of welfare schemes.
Examine how traditional knowledge and food practices can be integrated with modern nutritional strategies for sustainable health solutions in tribal regions.
GS2.imageDescription: A mobile health van (Poshan Vahini) reaching a remote tribal village.
GS2.trivia: Many tribal communities possess rich traditional knowledge about nutritious local foods and medicinal plants, which can be integrated into modern health programs.

GS2.headline: India Achieves Significant Milestone in Eliminating Lymphatic Filariasis Ahead of Global Targets
GS2.explainer:
• India has made remarkable progress in its fight against Lymphatic Filariasis (LF), also known as 'Hathipaon' or elephantiasis, achieving elimination targets in several endemic districts.
• This milestone positions India ahead of the global target for LF elimination set by the World Health Organization (WHO).
• The success is attributed to mass drug administration (MDA) campaigns, enhanced surveillance, and vector control measures.
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare lauded the collaborative efforts of state governments, health workers, and communities.
• The achievement has significant public health implications, reducing disability and improving the quality of life for millions.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Lymphatic Filariasis is a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD).
It is caused by parasitic worms transmitted by mosquitoes.
Mass Drug Administration (MDA) is a key strategy for LF elimination.
India's target was to eliminate LF as a public health problem by 2027 (ahead of WHO's 2030 target).
mainsPoints: Discuss the public health challenges posed by Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) like Lymphatic Filariasis in India.
Analyse the strategies and interventions that have contributed to India's success in controlling and eliminating LF.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of LF on affected communities and the benefits of its elimination.
Examine the role of national health programs, international collaborations, and community participation in achieving disease elimination goals.
GS2.imageDescription: A health worker administering medicine during a mass drug administration campaign.
GS2.trivia: Lymphatic Filariasis is one of the oldest and most debilitating tropical diseases, causing severe disfigurement and long-term disability.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Vocational Training Integration from School to Higher Education
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced significant progress in integrating vocational training across all levels of education, as envisioned by NEP 2020.
• New guidelines have been issued for schools to introduce vocational subjects from Class 6 onwards, including practical skills and internships.
• Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) are mandated to offer credit-based vocational courses and align curricula with industry needs.
• The move aims to enhance employability, reduce skill gaps, and foster an entrepreneurial mindset among students.
• Collaboration with industry partners and skill development councils is being strengthened to ensure relevance and quality of training.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 proposes vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
The policy aims to integrate vocational and academic streams.
Skill India Mission is a government initiative to train 40 crore people in various skills by 2022 (ongoing).
The National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) organizes qualifications according to a series of levels of knowledge, skills, and aptitude.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of vocational training integration in achieving the goals of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and addressing India's demographic dividend.
Analyse the challenges in implementing vocational education at scale, including infrastructure, faculty training, and industry collaboration.
Evaluate how a strong vocational education system can enhance youth employability, reduce skill gaps, and foster economic growth.
Examine the role of technology and innovative pedagogies in making vocational training more accessible and effective across diverse student populations.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in practical vocational training in a workshop.
GS2.trivia: Globally, countries like Germany and Switzerland have highly successful dual vocational training systems that combine classroom learning with on-the-job experience.

GS2.headline: Editorial: The Geopolitical Implications of Arctic Council Expansion and India's Role
GS2.explainer:

• It analyses the implications of new members or observers on the existing power dynamics and resource exploitation.
• India's increasing scientific and strategic interest in the Arctic, as an observer state, is highlighted.
• The editorial emphasizes the need for a balanced approach to Arctic governance, prioritizing environmental protection alongside economic opportunities.
• It calls for India to play a more proactive role in shaping sustainable policies for the rapidly changing polar region.
